entries.definition:
كتاب الميم

مكا

(مكث) الميم والكاف والثاء كلمةٌ تدلُّ على توقف وانتظار. ومَكَثَ مَكْثاً ومُكْثاً ورجل مَكِيث: رزينٌ غير عجول. ومَكَثَ ومَكُثَ والتمكُّث: الانتظار.

مَاعُون māʿūn [n.] variously interpreted as assistance to
neighbours in the form of lending household utensils; voluntary
and obligatory alms; obedience to the prescribed rules (107:6–7)
الَّذِينَ هُمْ يُرَاءُونَ وَيَمْنَعُونَ الْمَاعُونَ those who do good deeds in order to
be seen, [show off] and deny [to others] common kindnesses;

* الماعون name of Sura 107, Meccan sura, so-named because of the
  reference in verse 7 to ‘Common Kindness’.

مَعِين maʿīn [act. part. of root م/ع/ن m–ʿ–n or pass. part. of the
root ع/ي/ن ʿ–y–n] clear, running water, clear water from an open
spring (23:50) وَآوَيْنَاهُمَا إِلَى رَبْوَةٍ ذَاتِ قَرَارٍ وَمَعِينٍ and We gave them
shelter on a peaceful hillside with flowing water.
entries.definition_english:
مَاعُون māʿūn [n.] variously interpreted as assistance to
neighbours in the form of lending household utensils; voluntary
and obligatory alms; obedience to the prescribed rules (107:6–7)
الَّذِينَ هُمْ يُرَاءُونَ وَيَمْنَعُونَ الْمَاعُونَ those who do good deeds in order to
be seen, [show off] and deny [to others] common kindnesses;

* الماعون name of Sura 107, Meccan sura, so-named because of the

مَعِين maʿīn [act. part. of root م/ع/ن m–ʿ–n or pass. part. of the
root ع/ي/ن ʿ–y–n] clear, running water, clear water from an open
spring (23:50) وَآوَيْنَاهُمَا إِلَى رَبْوَةٍ ذَاتِ قَرَارٍ وَمَعِينٍ and We gave them
shelter on a peaceful hillside with flowing water.

entries.definition:
m–k–th to remain, to wait, to bide one’s time, to await the
outcome; to be self-restrained, to be calm. Of this root, three
forms occur seven times in the Qur’an: مَكَثَ makatha four times;
مُكْث mukth once and مَاكِثُون mākithūn twice.

مَكَثَ makatha u [v. intrans.] 1 to stay (20:10) امْكُثُوا إِنِّي آنَسْتُ نَارًا
stay here–I perceive a fire; perhaps I may bring you a brand from it
2 to tarry, to wait (27:22) فَمَكَثَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ but he did not stay away for
long 3 to remain (13:17) وَأَمَّا مَا يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ فَيَمْكُثُ 

فِي الْأَرْضِ but what is of benefit to humankind remains on earth.

مُكْث mukth [v. n.] the act of doing something surely,
unhurriedly, or in stages (17:106) وَقُرْآنًا فَرَقْنَاهُ لِتَقْرَأَهُ عَلَى النَّاسِ عَلَى مُكْثٍ
وَنَزَّلْنَاهُ تَنْزِيلًا it is a discourse that We have [divided up] revealed at
intervals, so that you recite it to people in stages; We have sent it
down little by little.

مَاكِثُونَ mākithūn [pl. of act. part. مَاكِث mākith] those who stay,
those who reside, those who bide (18:3) مَاكِثِينَ فِيهِ أَبَدًا in which they
will be staying for ever.
